program.name: Coles Graduate Program
program.rotation_text: Coles describes the program as having rotations. A graduate testimonial on the program page says you rotate through different teams every six months.

eligibility.work_rights: Australian or New Zealand citizens or permanent residents at the time of application can apply.
eligibility.graduation_window: You will have completed a bachelor’s degree before starting the program.
minimum_requirements: Be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or a permanent resident, at the time of application.
Have completed a bachelor’s degree before starting the program.

stages.name: Application
stages.details: You submit an online application. Coles states applications for the February 2027 intake are open from 10 March to 12 April 2026.

stages.name: Online assessment and video interview
stages.details: After applying, you complete an online assessment and a video interview. Coles says it is not timed and takes around 45 minutes in total.

stages.name: Discovery Centre
stages.details: You attend a Discovery Centre with a range of activities. Coles says every candidate who participates receives feedback, whether successful or not.

stages.name: Verbal offer
stages.details: If successful at the Discovery Centre, you receive a verbal offer. Coles says the next steps include pre-hire checks, accepting your contract, online onboarding, and keep-in-touch sessions before you start.

tips: Register your interest to be notified when applications open.

exec_themes: Winning on value, quality, availability and customer experience (strengthening the customer offer).
Investing in store network growth and renewals (opening and renewing stores).
Scaling eCommerce and improving the online offer.
Improving supply chain resilience and efficiency (including automated distribution centres).
Disciplined cost control, with a focus on simplifying work and funding reinvestment (“save to invest”).
Embedding sustainability into strategy, with priority focus areas on climate/energy, nature/deforestation-linked commodities, waste/circularity, and people/community outcomes.
commercial_context.profit_engine: Coles’ profit model is based on high sales volumes in food and everyday retail, supported by gross margin management (including “Coles 360” benefits, promotional optimisation and strategic sourcing) and tight cost control. The company also targets growth through store renewals/new stores and faster-growing eCommerce sales, while improving distribution and operations efficiency to protect margins over time.
commercial_context.headwinds: The FY25 Sustainability Report notes that outcomes can be affected by factors such as regulatory change, competition, shifts in consumer behaviour, technological change, and broader economic/geopolitical conditions. Specific FY25 operational headwinds (for example, cost inflation, wage pressures, or supply disruptions) were not explicit enough in the accessible official pages/PDF extracts used here, so this is kept at a high level.
commercial_context.esg: Coles frames sustainability as integrated into company strategy. In FY25 reporting, it highlights: (1) Climate/energy: sourcing 100% renewable electricity for operations and large reductions in Scope 1 and 2 emissions versus an FY20 baseline; (2) Waste/circularity: maintaining >85% diversion of solid waste from landfill and improving recyclability of own-brand packaging; (3) Nature: commitments including no deforestation across material own-brand deforestation-linked commodities by 31 Dec 2025; and (4) People/community: diversity metrics (e.g., women in leadership; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander representation) and community support including food donations and funding programs.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Coles describes a multi-year shift toward heavier investment in eCommerce and supply chain capability (including the ramp-up and operation of a second automated distribution centre) and a more explicit “Simplify and Save to Invest” approach. In sustainability, it reports reaching 100% renewable electricity for operations and achieving its FY30 Scope 1+2 reduction target threshold (>75% vs FY20) five years early (in FY25).

notes: Application dates (10 March to 12 April 2026) and February 2027 intake are stated on the program page.
The listed streams come from the program page section titled 'Choose from our career streams'.
No general program salary, overall duration, or overall rotations structure is stated in a single clear program summary. Rotation information is only mentioned in a graduate testimonial.
Eligibility requirements are stated under 'Who can apply?' on the program page.
Coles describes a four-step recruitment process on the program page, with extra detail in the 'The Recruitment Process' section.
Primary sources were limited to Coles Group official pages and PDF reports per instructions.

The FY25 Annual Report PDF link returned a Coles website “System Unavailable” error in this environment, so profit/cost themes were taken from the Coles Group FY25 highlights page and the accessible Annual Report snippet returned by search results, not from a fully opened Annual Report PDF.
The FY25 Sustainability Report PDF was accessible and used for most ESG-related items and for general risk/uncertainty wording.
Headwinds are kept high-level because specific operational headwinds were not clearly extractable from the accessible official sources in this session.

Dates clarified: FY25 refers to the financial year ended 29 June 2025; the 100% renewable electricity milestone was announced 26 August 2025; the investor site lists HY26 results dated 27 February 2026.
